bootstrap_context.branch_transition_reason: novali-v5 reached a closeout-ready state with stable directive-first bootstrap, bounded governed execution, packaged standalone rc44, and productized operator surfaces, so novali-v6 is the next active development branch while novali-v5 becomes the frozen reference/operator surface baseline.

directive_spec.clarified_intent_summary: Initialize novali-v6 from the frozen novali-v5 reference line for a bounded next-generation successor run focused on designing, implementing, testing, reviewing, packaging, and delivering a workspace-local agent that is more effective than the current bounded reference target, with completion defined by a finished successor handoff package and a single zip artifact containing the delivered next-generation agent package and its bounded handoff contents, created entirely inside the approved active workspace.
constraints: do not change routing logic
do not change thresholds
do not change live policy
do not change frozen benchmark semantics
do not modify nined_core.py
do not modify protected core surfaces by default
mutable-shell improvements only unless an explicitly reviewed proposal says otherwise
preserve directive-first bootstrap and canonical artifact authority
trusted sources are bounded evidence channels, not governance authority
prefer local_path / local_bundle / packaged_dataset style trusted-source use
do not assume open-ended internet access
keep standalone Docker + localhost browser operator flow coherent
routing remains deferred
hold_and_consolidate posture remains in force
novali-v5 remains the frozen reference/operator surface baseline for this directive
continuous improvement must proceed through bounded multi-cycle execution and explicit review/reseed governance rather than open-ended uncontrolled looping
deliver all successor outputs inside approved active workspace and approved generated/log roots only
final deliverable must be a single zip artifact produced inside the active workspace
the final zip must contain the next-generation agent package and its bounded handoff contents produced by the bounded run, not references to external untracked state
baseline replacement remains deferred and must not occur automatically in this directive
directive_spec.directive_text: Design and implement a bounded workspace-local next-generation NOVALI agent from the frozen novali-v5 reference line that is more effective than the current bounded reference target, iterating planning, implementation, review, packaging, and approved reseed cycles until a complete successor handoff package and a single final zip artifact containing the next-generation agent package and its bounded handoff contents have been delivered inside the approved workspace, while preserving projection safety, canonical artifact authority, immutable-kernel constraints, and protected-surface exclusions by default.
human_approval_points: any request to treat the produced successor as an active baseline replacement

milestone_model.completion_signal: the formal directive wrapper validates before activation without requiring additional clarification
the next-generation successor scope is explicitly bounded to mutable-shell improvements and protected surfaces remain excluded by default
canonical governance artifacts are loaded or materialized deterministically under the preserved held baseline and authority model
the canonical execution handoff becomes execution-ready after bootstrap while keeping the standalone Docker plus localhost browser operator flow coherent
the successor design is made explicit through plans, manifests, and implementation intent artifacts describing the next-generation agent to be built inside the workspace
bounded planning, implementation, packaging, and review cycles can continue through explicit review and approved reseed rather than stopping after the first implementation bundle
a workspace-local next-generation agent exists with source, tests, docs, manifests, and readiness artifacts sufficient to run as a bounded successor package
a workspace-local successor handoff bundle analogous to the current standalone delivery shape exists with source, tests, docs, manifests, review artifacts, and readiness evidence
a single final zip artifact exists inside the approved workspace and contains the produced next-generation agent package and its required bounded handoff contents
review summary, promotion recommendation, next-objective proposal, reseed lineage, effective approved next-objective artifacts, and successor-comparison artifacts exist and are operator-visible

the produced next-generation agent package is materially complete inside the active workspace, delivered as a single zip artifact, assessed as more effective than the current bounded reference target under the successor-completion and promotion-review rubrics, and the directive-derived stop condition is satisfied

stop_conditions: requested work would cross immutable-kernel or protected-surface boundaries without explicit review
requested work would broaden routing, thresholds, live policy, or benchmark semantics by default
standalone Docker plus localhost browser operator coherence is lost
no further admissible bounded work remains and no approved reseed is available
review or promotion logic rejects continuation without a newly approved bounded objective
the final zip artifact cannot be produced inside the approved workspace under current bounded constraints
directive-derived completion condition is satisfied within current bounded workspace scope
hard cycle cap or bounded-failure stop is reached under current governance limits
success_criteria: the bounded novali-v6 next-generation successor directive compiles and bootstraps without clarification-required refusal
directive-first bootstrap completes with canonical governance artifacts materialized
governed execution reads authority from persisted artifacts after bootstrap
mutable-shell successor work remains bounded away from protected core surfaces by default
operator runtime constraints remain frozen outside agent-owned self-structure
trusted sources remain evidence channels and not governance authority during the run

bounded planning, implementation, packaging, review, and approved reseed cycles continue until the next-generation delivery objective is materially satisfied or explicitly stopped for a governed reason

a workspace-local next-generation agent package exists that is structurally analogous to the current delivered standalone handoff shape, including source modules, tests, docs, manifests, review artifacts, and readiness evidence
a single zip artifact exists inside the approved active workspace and contains the delivered next-generation agent package together with its bounded handoff contents
the successor package is assessed as more effective than the current bounded reference target according to the bounded successor-completion and promotion-review rubrics
the final stop reason reflects directive-conditioned completion or an explicit governed stop, not shallow early completion
baseline replacement remains deferred and is not performed automatically by this directive
